title: Blowout Preventer Shearing Ram
abstract: A blowout preventer (BOP) includes a main body that includes a bore extending therethrough and opposing shear rams configured to shear a tubular positioned within the bore. The opposing shear rams are configured to guide the tubular to be positioned within the bore in one of a plurality of recesses of the opposing shear rams upon engagement between the tubular and the opposing shear rams.
claims: Claims What is Claimed is: 1. A blowout preventer (BOP) comprising: a main body comprising a bore extending therethrough; and opposing shear rams configured to shear a tubular positioned within the bore, wherein the opposing shear rams are configured to guide the tubular to be positioned within the bore in one of a plurality of recesses of the opposing shear rams upon engagement between the tubular and the opposing shear rams. 2. The BOP of claim 1, wherein a first shear ram of the opposing shear rams comprises a center blade edge, and a second shear ram of the opposing shear rams comprises a center recess positioned opposite the center blade edge of the first shear ram along a longitudinal axis. 3. The BOP of claim 2, wherein the center recess, upon receipt of the tubular, is configured to maintain a position of the tubular along a center axis of the opposing rams to enable the center blade edge of the first shear ram to shear the tubular during engagement between the tubular and the opposing shear rams. 4. The BOP of claim 2, wherein the first shear ram comprises recesses positioned at opposite sides of the center blade edge along a lateral axis, the second shear ram comprises blade edges positioned at opposite sides of the center recess along the lateral axis, and each of the recesses of the first shear ram is positioned opposite one of the blade edges of the second shear ram along the longitudinal axis. 5. The BOP of claim 4, wherein each of the recesses of the first shear ram, upon receipt of the tubular, is configured to maintain the tubular in a respective position offset from a center axis of the opposing rams to enable one of the blade edges of the second shear ram to shear the tubular during engagement between the tubular and the opposing shear rams. 6. The BOP of claim 4, wherein the recesses of the first shear ram are positioned symmetrically about the center blade edge along the lateral axis, and the blade edges of the second shear ram are positioned symmetrically about the center recess along the lateral axis. 7. The BOP of claim 2, wherein the first shear ram is an upper shear ram, and the second shear ram is a lower shear ram. 8. A pair of opposing shear rams for a blowout preventer (BOP), the pair of opposing shear rams comprising: a first shear ram comprising a first blade edge and first recesses positioned at opposite sides of the first blade edge along a lateral axis; and a second shear ram comprising a second recess and second blade edges positioned at opposite sides of the second recess along the lateral axis, wherein the second recess is positioned opposite the first blade edge of the first shear ram along a longitudinal axis, and each of the second blade edges is positioned opposite a respective one of the first recesses of the first shear ram along the longitudinal axis, such that one of the first recesses or the second recess is configured to receive a tubular extending through the BOP during engagement between the tubular and the first shear ram, the second shear ram, or both. 9. The pair of opposing shear rams of claim 8, wherein the first shear ram comprises tapered surfaces angled inwardly toward one of the first recesses along the longitudinal axis, and the tapered surfaces are configured to guide the tubular toward one of the first recesses upon engagement with the tubular. 10. The pair of opposing shear rams of claim 8, wherein the second shear ram comprises notches extending from each of the second blade edges toward a respective lateral side of the second ram at a respective angle with respect to the lateral axis and the longitudinal axis. 11. The pair of opposing shear rams of claim 10, wherein the second shear ram comprises lateral surfaces each extending from a respective lateral side toward one of the second blade edges along the longitudinal axis, and the lateral surfaces are angled to guide the tubular toward one of the first recesses of the first shear ram upon engagement with the tubular. 12. The pair of opposing shear rams of claim 8, wherein the first shear ram and the second shear ram are configured to move toward one another to engage one another. 13. The pair of opposing shear rams of claim 12, wherein the first shear ram comprises a first blade section comprising the first blade edge and the first recesses, the second shear ram comprises a second blade section comprising the second recess and the second blade edges, and the first blade section is configured to contact and engage the second blade section when the first shear ram and the second shear ram engage one another. 14. The pair of opposing shear rams of claim 13, wherein the first blade section comprises a first interior surface, the second blade section comprises a second interior surface, the first interior surface is configured to contact and engage the second interior surface during engagement between the first shear ram and the second shear ram, and the first interior surface is positioned above the second interior surface along a vertical axis. 15. A blowout preventer (BOP) comprises: a bore extending therethrough and configured to receive a tubular; a first shear ram comprising a first body and a first blade section extending from the first body along a longitudinal axis, wherein the first blade section comprises a center blade edge and off-center recesses positioned at opposite sides of the center blade edge along a lateral axis; and a second shear ram comprising a second body and a second blade section extending from the second body along the longitudinal axis, wherein the second blade section comprises a center recess and off-center blade edges positioned at opposite sides of the center recess along the lateral axis, wherein the first shear ram and the second shear ram are each translatable along the longitudinal axis to engage the tubular, and the first shear ram, the second shear ram, or both, are configured to guide the tubular toward one of the off-center recesses or the center recess during engagement with the tubular. 16. The BOP of claim 15, wherein the center blade edge of the first shear ram is positioned opposite the center recess of the second shear ram along the longitudinal axis, and each of the off-center recesses of the first shear ram is positioned opposite a respective one of the off- center blade edges of the second shear ram along the longitudinal axis. 17. The BOP of claim 15, wherein the first shear ram and the second shear ram are each translatable along the longitudinal axis to engage one another, the first shear ram comprises an arm extending from the first body along the longitudinal axis, the arm is offset from the first blade section along a vertical axis to form a slot spanning between the first blade section and the arm, and a portion of the second blade section of the second shear ram is configured to insert into the slot during engagement between the first shear ram and the second shear ram to seal the bore. 18. The BOP of claim 17, wherein the portion of the second blade section comprises the center recess and the off-center blade edges. 19. The BOP of claim 15, wherein each of the first body of the first shear ram and the second body of the second shear ram comprises a respective groove configured to receive a respective seal element to block fluid flow across the first shear ram, the second shear ram, or both. 20. The BOP of claim 15, wherein the first shear ram comprises respective tapered surfaces angled inwardly with respect to the longitudinal axis and extending toward the off-center recesses, the second shear ram comprises respective tapered surfaces angled inwardly with respect to the longitudinal axis and extending toward the center recess, the respective tapered surfaces are configured to guide the tubular into the center recess or one of the off-center recesses during engagement with the tubular, and the respective tapered surfaces are configured to capture the tubular to maintain a position of the tubular within the bore upon engagement with the tubular.
